title: Marty Supreme Production Designer Jack Fisk Interview: Awards Chatter
text: Jack Fisk, 80-year-old legendary production designer, was interviewed for The Hollywood Reporter's Awards Chatter podcast. Fisk discussed his extensive five-decade career designing production for acclaimed films including Badlands, Carrie, Days of Heaven, Mulholland Drive, There Will Be Blood, The Revenant, and Killers of the Flower Moon. The interview covered his journey to Hollywood in 1970 from Virginia alongside David Lynch, his collaborations with major filmmakers like Terrence Malick (eight films), Brian De Palma, Paul Thomas Anderson, and Martin Scorsese, and his relationship with actress Sissy Spacek, whom he met on the Badlands set and married in 1974. Fisk received his fourth Oscar nomination for his work on Marty Supreme, the Josh Safdie film starring Timothée Chalamet, with observers noting he appears poised to win for the first time. The discussion delved into Marty Supreme, examining how Fisk recreated 1950s New York through research and creative vision, marking only his second production design work set in the city.
